name: Thorn Writes to Serel
description: Thorn has written a letter. Not an official communication, not a strategic assessment, not a report on the ceasefire status -- a letter. He wants it delivered to Commander Serel at Fort Sky Watch. The ceasefire has been holding for months, the bone fields have had their first cross-border gathering, and he has things to say that do not belong in a military dispatch.
accept_text: He has the letter already written. He holds it for a moment before handing it over, which is the pause of someone who has considered not sending it and decided to send it anyway. 'It's not official,' he says. 'There is a separate official communication through the standard diplomatic channel. This is the other thing.' He hands it to you. It is sealed. 'What the ceasefire has become is not what either command expected. The bone fields gathering. The intelligence officers meeting Noss is arranging. The research teams.' He is quiet. 'I have been in communication with Serel through official channels for four months. She responds to the official communications with official responses, which is correct. But I have been on the eastern front for two years. I have spoken to Serel through a channel wall in the dark while Abominations moved forty meters away. That is a different kind of acquaintance than the official correspondence would suggest.' He looks at the letter. 'I have things to say about what has happened that I am not able to put in a military dispatch without it becoming a report about itself rather than a communication from one person to another.' He pauses. 'Tell her -- if she asks -- that I am not requesting anything. I am informing. There is a distinction.' He almost smiles. 'She will know what I mean.'
complete_text: She takes the letter. She reads it standing up, which is how she reads most things. You watch her face while she reads. Nothing moves. Then something does -- not obvious, not dramatic, the kind of shift that happens in a face when a sentence lands correctly. She folds the letter. She does not put it away immediately. She holds it. 'He says he is not requesting anything,' she says. 'He is right that I know what he means.' She looks out at the bone fields in the distance -- you can see them from the Fort Sky Watch parapet, two kilometers east, the trees at the edge of the Terran side. 'The ceasefire was a military arrangement. What has happened since is not a military arrangement.' She is quiet. 'The Day changed the calculation. The Grid is whole. The reason both sides are in the Wastes -- the crystal, the Aetherium, the strategic value of the lattice -- the meaning of all of that is different now.' She looks at the letter. 'Tell him: I received it. Tell him I am going to write back, which is also not an official response.' She pauses. 'Also tell him that I said yes on the morning of the Day, which he already knows because he can feel it in the network, but some things should be said in words rather than resonance.' She looks at the bone fields. 'The ceasefire was the beginning of something. I did not know, when I agreed to it in the dark, what it was the beginning of. I am starting to see the shape of it.' She folds her hands around the letter. 'Tell him the shape is good.'
